headline: The AI Reckoning Begins: Companies Face the 'Last Mile' Problem as Scaling Shifts from Hype to Hard Work

stories.title: The 'Last Mile' Problem Is Slowing AI Transformation
stories.summary: Most companies have pilots and tools access but literally can't move beyond that—the real obstacle isn't tech quality but actually changing how work gets done.
stories.takeaway: Companies are hitting a wall: pilots everywhere but real business value requires redesigning entire workflows, not just plugging AI into old processes.
stories.strategyTake: Workflow redesign beats tool buying. The 80/20 rule matters: only 20% is technology, other 80% is people & process rethinking.

stories.title: AI M&A Enters Industrial Phase as Companies Hunt for Data & Computing Assets
stories.summary: Hyperscalers are consolidating chip design, data centers, and AI talent through strategic M&A, echoing early internet era playbooks.
stories.takeaway: This isn't just competition—it's companies building full-stack ecosystems. The winners will be those with control over data, models, AND computing efficiency.
stories.strategyTake: Strategic M&A is the new playbook—focus on infrastructure readiness & differentiated computing efficiency, not flashy AI startups.

stories.title: How AI Is Reshaping White-Collar Work (and It's Not What You Think)
stories.summary: New research shows AI isn't cutting jobs uniformly but fundamentally recalibrating skill needs—only the work is changing, not necessarily the headcount.
stories.takeaway: Employees worked faster, took on broader tasks, and extended work hours—AI intensified work rather than reducing it, flipping the automation narrative.
stories.strategyTake: The real shift: people who orchestrate AI agents, verify high-stakes outputs, and set strategic goals will define the new white-collar tier.

stories.title: Agents Aren't Ready for Prime Time (Yet), But Leaders Need to Prep Anyway
stories.summary: MIT Sloan's latest findings show agentic AI still has hallucinations and security issues, but companies should start building infrastructure now for 5-year deployment window.
stories.takeaway: Lowkey: agents are hyped but not ready. Smart move is treating 2026 as the 'level-set' year—maximize current AI while building for the agent future.
stories.strategyTake: Don't oversell agentic AI internally. Focus on workflow redesign & enterprise-scale use cases first, then layer in agents when maturity hits.

watchNext.title: Why AI Adoption Stalls According to Industry Data
watchNext.why: Pulls back the curtain on the real reason pilots never scale—it's not tech, it's employee anxiety about relevance & org readiness

watchNext.title: Supply Chain Planning 2026: Why AI Alone Isn't Enough
watchNext.why: BCG breaks down how leaders actually separate from laggards—it's not better models, it's how humans apply capabilities & evaluate tradeoffs
